'''Jordan Dezaria''' (born 6 November 1996) is a [[French people|French]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and for the [[Leigh Centurions]] and [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has spent time on loan at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
Dezaria was born in [[Avignon]], [[Salon-de-Provence]], [[France]].

==Career==
===Catalans Dragons===
He made his Super League debut in a match against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in June 2016.<ref name="GUARDIAN VERDICT: Warrington Wolves 20 Catalans Dragons 18">{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/14565461.GUARDIAN_VERDICT__Warrington_Wolves_20_Catalans_Dragons_18/|title=GUARDIAN VERDICT: Warrington Wolves 20 Catalans Dragons 18|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=2016-06-18|accessdate=2016-09-14}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
In October 2017 Dezaria signed for the [[Leigh Centurions]] on a two-year contract.<ref>[https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/blog/2017/10/18/french-duo-sign-up-for-the-centurions/ French Duo Sign Up For The Centurions]</ref>

===Catalans Dragons (re-join)===
In December 2020 Catalans re-signed Dezaria along with [[Gavin Marguerite]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/194-7524-catalans-sign-jordan-dezaria-and-gavin-marguerite/|title=CATALANS SIGN JORDAN DEZARIA AND GAVIN MARGUERITE|website=www.catalansdragons.com|accessdate=13 Dec 2020}}</ref>
===Castleford Tigers (loan)===
On 15 April 2025, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Dezaria on a one-month loan from Catalans.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=15 April 2025 |title=Catalans Dragons prop answers Castleford Tigers SOS with loan move |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/catalans-dragons-prop-answers-castleford-tigers-sos-with-loan-move-5083892 |access-date=15 April 2025 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

He made his debut on 17 April 2025 in the away Round 8 fixture at [[Wakefield Trinity]].

On 6 May 2025 it was reported that he had been recalled by [[Catalans Dragons]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 May 2025|title=Castleford Tigers forward departs as hint dropped on future transfer|author=Ben Olawumi|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-forward-departs-as-hint-dropped-on-future-transfer}}</ref>

On 15 October 2025 it was reported that [[Catalans Dragons|Catalans]] had released him<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=15 Oct 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Catalans Dragons release long-serving forward from contract|url=https://www.totalrl.com/catalans-dragons-release-long-serving-forward-from-contract/}}</ref> 

===Hull KR===
On 28 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed a 3-year deal with [[Hull KR]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=28 Oct 2025|title=Hull KR sign France international Dezaria|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5ypkgel9kro}}</ref>

===St Helens (loan)===
On 31 March 2026 it was reported that he had joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on a short term loan.
He made his début on 3 April 2026, coming off the bench for the 34-24 win over [[Wigan Warriors]].

==International career==
He was selected in France 9s squad for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].<ref>{{cite web|date=2 October 2019|title=La liste des joueurs pour la Coupe du Monde dévoilée!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/la-liste-des-joueurs-pour-la-coupe-du-monde-devoilee/|publisher=FFRXIII|accessdate=1 November 2019|language=French}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2019|title=XIII de France: Romain Puso remplace Lilian Albert, forfait!|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/actualite-a-la-une/xiii-de-france-romain-puso-remplace-lilian-albert-forfait/|publisher=FFR XIII|accessdate=1 November 2019|language=French}}</ref>
Dezaria scored the opening try for France in their [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign as they defeated Greece 34-12.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63293849|title=Rugby League World Cup: France 34-12 Greece - Les Bleus beat debutants|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
